---
title: "Python for Beginners"
number-sections: false
---

::: {.hero}
# Python for Beginners

A hands-on Python course for curious beginners.

Learn by writing real code, solving short exercises, opening hints only when needed, and building small projects.

[Start the course →](setup.qmd){.btn .btn-primary}
[Go to Lesson 1](lessons/python_lesson_1.qmd){.btn .btn-secondary}
:::

## What you will learn

::: {.feature-grid}

::: {.feature-card}
### Write real Python

Create and run `.py` files in Positron.
:::

::: {.feature-card}
### Build interactive programs

Use `input()`, variables and `print()` to make programs respond to the user.
:::

::: {.feature-card}
### Make decisions

Use `if`, `else` and `elif` to make programs choose between options.
:::

::: {.feature-card}
### Repeat actions

Use loops to build quizzes, games and practice tools.
:::

::: {.feature-card}
### Work with lists

Store names, scores, shopping items and other collections.
:::

::: {.feature-card}
### Build projects

Make games, calculators, quizzes and small apps.
:::

::: {.lesson-card}
### Mini Project 1

Build a quiz game using variables, input and `if`.

[Open Mini Project 1](projects/mini_project_1_quiz_game.qmd)
:::

::: {.lesson-card}
### Mini Project 2

Build a guess-the-number game using loops.

[Open Mini Project 2](projects/mini_project_2_guess_the_number.qmd)
:::

::: {.lesson-card}
### Lesson 6

Work with strings using f-strings, `.lower()`, `.strip()` and `len()`.

[Open Lesson 6](lessons/python_lesson_6.qmd)
:::

::: {.lesson-card}
### Lesson 7

Create reusable code using functions.

[Open Lesson 7](lessons/python_lesson_7.qmd)
:::

::: {.lesson-card}
### Lesson 8

Use randomness to create dice rolls, random choices and games.

[Open Lesson 8](lessons/python_lesson_8.qmd)
:::

::: {.lesson-card}
### Mini Project 3

Build a dice game using functions, loops and randomness.

[Open Mini Project 3](projects/mini_project_3_dice_game.qmd)
:::

::: {.lesson-card}
### Lesson 9

Save and read information using text files.

[Open Lesson 9](lessons/python_lesson_9.qmd)
:::

::: {.lesson-card}
### Lesson 10

Use dictionaries to store labelled information.

[Open Lesson 10](lessons/python_lesson_10.qmd)
:::

::: {.lesson-card}
### Lesson 11

Read errors, debug calmly and handle invalid input.

[Open Lesson 11](lessons/python_lesson_11.qmd)
:::

::: {.lesson-card}
### Mini Project 4

Build a flashcard trainer that checks answers and saves scores.

[Open Mini Project 4](projects/mini_project_4_flashcard_trainer.qmd)
:::

:::

## How to use the course

::: {.steps}

**1. Read the short explanation**  
Each lesson introduces only one or two new ideas.

**2. Type the examples yourself**  
Do not just copy and paste. Typing helps you notice how code works.

**3. Try the exercises before opening hints**  
The hints are there to help, not to replace thinking.

**4. Check the solution only at the end**  
There are many ways to solve a coding problem. Compare your solution with the example.

**5. Try the challenge**  
The challenges are where the lesson turns into real programming.

:::

## Course path

::: {.lesson-grid}

::: {.lesson-card}
### Setup

Install the tools and learn how to move around Positron.

[Open setup](setup.qmd)
:::

::: {.lesson-card}
### Getting Started with Positron

Learn the editor, console, terminal, explorer, variables and plots panes.

[Open Positron intro](lessons/positron_intro.qmd)
:::

::: {.lesson-card}
### Lesson 1

Your first Python program: `print()`, variables, strings and arithmetic.

[Open Lesson 1](lessons/python_lesson_1.qmd)
:::

::: {.lesson-card}
### Lesson 2

Make Python interactive with `input()`.

[Open Lesson 2](lessons/python_lesson_2.qmd)
:::

::: {.lesson-card}
### Lesson 3

Use `if`, `else` and `elif`.

[Open Lesson 3](lessons/python_lesson_3.qmd)
:::

::: {.lesson-card}
### Lesson 4

Repeat code with `while` loops.

[Open Lesson 4](lessons/python_lesson_4.qmd)
:::

::: {.lesson-card}
### Lesson 5

Use lists and `for` loops.

[Open Lesson 5](lessons/python_lesson_5.qmd)
:::

::: {.lesson-card}
### Projects

Build larger programs using everything you have learned.

[Open projects](projects/index.qmd)
:::

:::

## Suggested pace

One lesson per session works well.

A good session is about **45–60 minutes**:

- 10–15 minutes reading and trying examples,
- 20–30 minutes on exercises,
- 10–15 minutes on challenges or debugging.

::: {.callout-tip}
## Best rule

Do not worry about finishing quickly. The goal is to understand what your code is doing.
:::

For each lesson:

1. Read the explanation.
2. Run the examples.
3. Try the exercises.
4. Open hints only when needed.
5. Check the solutions at the end.
6. Try at least one challenge.

## Ready?

Start with [Setup](setup.qmd).
